text: I write as the account holder for the Family Plus plan (account ending 3214) to escalate an unresolved billing discrepancy that is causing immediate financial strain. On 12 March 2026 I contacted your billing team and spoke with Ms Patel (case ref 842173) about a duplicated line charge of £74.50 that appeared on the statement dated 05 March 2026; I was advised a correction would be applied within 5 working days but no credit has been posted and a further direct debit of £298.92 was taken on 20 March 2026. This is the second formal attempt to have the matter corrected—my earlier email of 14 March 2026 remains unanswered—and the continuing uncertainty is affecting our household budget and the children’s access to school services. Please investigate, reverse the erroneous charge, provide a corrected bill and a written confirmation of the refund within 48 hours; if you cannot meet that timescale, advise a named contact and escalation route so I can take the matter further with confidence.

I am a small business owner and my case has been mishandled for too long. On 2 March I reported a billing error where a duplicate charge of £310 appeared on the account; I was promised a refund by 12 March by adviser Harpreet. That date passed and I called again on 15 March, was transferred three times, given two different reference numbers and told someone would call back — no call came. I escalated on 20 March and spoke to Emma who said the refund was authorised, yet my bank statement shows nothing. This complaint has been passed around with contradictory information and no resolution. I have exhausted the usual routes and now request a named manager to review the file, confirm the refund in writing, and provide a timeline for when I will see the money. If I do not receive a satisfactory reply within five working days I will submit a formal complaint to the ombudsman and include all correspondence.

I am writing to report a recurring fault with the handset provided when I switched to your service on 04/03/2026. The device (Samsung A53, IMEI ending 1123) exhibits persistent issues: the microphone intermittently cuts out during calls, the screen flickers on wake, and the battery drains from 80% to 25% within three hours of light use. I reported these faults to customer support on 05/03/2026 (call handled by Raj Patel, incident reference REF987654) and was advised to perform a factory reset. I completed the reset as instructed but the defects remain. This is my second formal contact about the handset; the previous troubleshooting has not resolved the matter. Given the symptoms and prior diagnostics, I request either a replacement device or a written specification of the next technical steps you will take, together with a timescale for resolution. Please escalate to your device support team and respond with a clear remedy within ten working days.

I am a student living on a tight budget and I am beyond frustrated with the unexplained charges on my account. I contacted you once already on 5 March 2026 via web chat (reference REF-45217) and spoke to Aisha, who said she would 'pass this to billing' and that someone would call — nobody has called and the extra charges remain.

The issue: my February invoice shows "administration fee" £9.99, "connection fee" £3.50 and an additional "contract adjustment" £4.20. I have combed through my contract and the tariff sheet I signed on 12 September 2025 and none of these items appear. I have never agreed to a connection fee after my SIM swap in January, and the so-called adjustment does not match any usage anomaly.

I have attached (to the web form) screenshots of my contract page and the invoice. To make this painfully clear: my monthly plan is £12.00. The bill shows 12 + 9.99 + 3.50 + 4.20 = £29.69. I am a student; an unexpected extra £17.69 is not trivial. I need a full breakdown of each of these fees: why they were applied, the exact date each fee was incurred, and the legal or contractual basis for each charge. I want the incorrect charges removed and the money refunded to my debit card. If they are valid, show me the section in my contract or a screenshot of the change I allegedly accepted.

This is my second attempt. I am not prepared to tolerate vague promises that 'billing will investigate' without concrete answers. Please provide a full itemised response and a confirmation of refund or reversal within 14 days so I can plan my monthly budget accordingly.

Order ID: SW-20260302-112. Timeline and current position below.

I switched my fibre service to you on 2 March 2026. The engineer visit was scheduled for 10 March and completed on that date, but since then the connection has suffered intermittent dropouts during peak hours (typically between 18:00 and 22:00) and sustained latency spikes up to 350 ms. I raised this as a fault on 12 March (ticket 209344) and spoke with Raj on 15 March who said the issue would be escalated to network operations. No further updates have been provided and the ticket shows no progress in the online portal.

To be clear: this is my second attempt to get an update. The lack of communication is causing disruptions to evening work calls and home schooling video sessions. Please supply a full timeline of actions taken to diagnose the problem, the person or team responsible for the next steps, and a realistic ETA for resolution. If an on-site visit or remedial works are required, provide available appointment slots within the next five business days. I would appreciate a written update by close of business on 29 March 2026.

Hi — I need this cleared up properly. Over the last three weeks I have called and sent messages about mysterious extra charges on my bill and keep getting the runaround. First call was 04 March with Leah, who said she would remove an itemised ‘service admin fee’ of £3.50 and refund it; second call on 11 March with Daniel who told me it was a one-off and wouldn’t recur; third call via web chat on 18 March with Aisha who promised an investigation and case number (V-1803-55). The amount keeps appearing: a small sum, I accept, but I am on a low-income support package and these additions add up. I am fed up of having to explain my circumstances repeatedly to different people and being told different things each time. Can you please either send me a clear breakdown showing what each charge is for and why it was applied, or cancel any fees that were applied without my consent and process a refund for the last four weeks? I don’t need urgent action; I can wait while you sort it properly. I do need an end to the circular talking, though — give me a single point of contact or a final decision so I can stop chasing this every few days.

I am writing as the primary account holder for a family bundle with four lines (account reference 79-3045). Over the past three weeks I have identified a sequence of fees which were not disclosed at the point of renewal: a so-called 'admin charge' of £17.95 applied on 03 March, and a recurring 'service management' fee of £4.99 applied from 10 March. I have called customer services on 4 separate occasions (14 March, 17 March, 21 March and 23 March) and spoken with agents Rina Patel and a supervisor in billing, who logged references BIL-1423 and BIL-2147. Each time I was told that the charges were legitimate and that I would receive an itemised breakdown by email; that breakdown has not arrived.

As a parent managing multiple lines, transparent billing is essential. I require a full explanation in writing of the nature and contractual basis for these fees, a full refund of all unagreed charges applied since 1 March 2026, and confirmation that no such fees will be levied in future without express consent. Please respond within seven working days with the documentation I requested. If you cannot substantiate these charges, I expect them removed immediately and a written apology sent to my email address on file.

This is my first contact regarding cancellation of the family mobile bundle attached to account ending 9421. On 18 March 2026 I attempted to cancel via the online portal; the interface accepted my request but did not issue any confirmation or final date, and the dashboard still shows the service as active. As a parent organising household accounts I need a formal written confirmation of contract termination, the effective end date, and an itemised final bill showing any early-termination charges (if applicable). Please treat this as my formal notice to end the service and send the necessary documentation to the registered email address within 14 days so I can make alternative arrangements.

So my bill went up mid-contract, did it. I signed an 18-month agreement on 15 September 2025 for £22 per month and on 1 March the direct debit increased to £27. I rang three times over the last fortnight — spoke to Darren on 05/03, then Pat on 11/03, and a different advisor (no name given) on 18/03 — who all kept telling me it's 'an update to terms' and couldn't explain why my fixed-price period was seemingly ignored. I’m not tech-savvy and I don’t want to be fussing with apps or account settings; I expect clear answers when I call. If the contract is being altered while I’m halfway through it, that’s not right. Please reverse the rise to the original £22 and confirm you will honour the remainder of my agreement, or tell me plainly why that cannot happen. I’m not asking for miracles, just fairness.

You sold me the wrong package. On 15/03/2026 your sales rep Neil recorded my order as a residential 100Mbps line when I explicitly said this is for my café and I need the business 200/200 product. I raised this on 18/03 with customer services and got ticket 992733, but nothing has been corrected. My till depends on a stable upload and the current setup drops during busy periods, costing sales. I need the order swapped to the correct business tariff and confirmation of installation dates within three working days. If you cannot fix this immediately, provide a clear compensation plan for lost trading days and move me to a temporary business-grade connection until the permanent fix is in place.

So you cancelled my line on 2 March 2026, which is helpful, because I expressly asked for the family plan to be pared back. Your website even sent me a cancellation confirmation reference: CF-83912. Imagine my surprise to see the direct debit taken on 25 March for £42.50 for the very line I asked to remove. I appreciate systems can be slow; I also appreciate that charging for a service that was confirmed as cancelled is not best practice. Please reverse the payment and confirm the refund date, and while you are at it update the account so the rest of the family plan is charged correctly. I am a parent juggling school runs and work; handling billing errors offers me no pleasure, only inconvenience. I expect a correction on the next statement and a brief note explaining what went wrong.

I am contacting you for the first time about a critical failure that endangers my safety. I am registered as a vulnerable customer under the Equality Act and rely on my mobile to connect to carers and the telecare service. On 21/03/2026 I discovered that outgoing calls to my nominated emergency contact and the telecare number fail to connect; the handset shows ‘Network Registered’ but calls do not complete and SMS delivery reports are not received. There has been no prior contact with your team because I only became aware of the issue when an expected call from my carer did not come through at 18:00. I require priority handling: a same‑day engineer visit or a temporary loan handset and an immediate check of my account provisioning. Your duty of care to vulnerable customers and the regulatory guidance around prioritising people in such circumstances mean this cannot wait. Please treat this as an urgent safety escalation and confirm within two hours how you will restore my ability to contact emergency and care services. If you are unable to provide an immediate remedy, advise what interim safeguards you will put in place so my carers can reach me.

I am 78 and have been a customer since 2010. I am writing because the switch-off of your 3G network is preventing me from using my emergency pendant and my mobile phone in my area — a steady loss of service that began on 10/02/2026. I have called your support line six times, most recently on 08/03/2026 when I spoke with Hannah in the technical team who suggested buying a new 4G handset; I told her I cannot manage that without clear in-person help. On 12/03/2026 I was transferred and spoke with Samir who promised a call back from a local engineer; that call never arrived. I have explained repeatedly that my pendant, which I use daily, is 3G only and is the reason I kept this particular SIM plan. I do not understand the technology and each automated menu leaves me more anxious. I have exhausted your standard channels and I now request escalation to a manager and a clear written explanation of what support you will provide to ensure my pendant is replaced or that I am offered a suitable alternative at no extra cost. If you cannot provide immediate assistance I will take this matter to Ombudsman Services: Communications. Please do not put me through another scripted diagnostic flow. I need a named manager and a direct contact number to arrange a simple, practical resolution within five working days.

My retail business depends on the internet connection supplied under contract number MXS-4817, signed 15/11/2023. Due to the forced surrender of our lease and store closure on 20/02/2026, I instructed termination of service and was surprised to see a termination charge of £720 on the final statement issued 02/03/2026. I raised this matter previously on 05/03/2026 with Sophie in customer accounts (reference 05032026-S), who said she would 'look into the contract terms and escalation', yet nothing substantive has been provided and the invoice remains outstanding. I require a transparent accounting of how you have calculated the £720 figure: show the monthly fee used, remaining term months, any promotional discounts that should be apportioned, and evidence of any contractual clause your team relies on to claim this sum. As a small business, I note industry practice obliges suppliers to mitigate losses rather than simply levy full remaining contract charges; please confirm what mitigation steps you have taken to reassign or resell the line. I am prepared to pay a reasonable reconciliation if properly justified, but until I receive the requested documentation I must dispute the charge. Please provide the full breakdown and legal justification within five business days. If the company cannot substantiate the fee I will seek resolution through the Ombudsman and, if necessary, pursue recovery through the small claims process. This is my second attempt to obtain an answer; continued silence is not acceptable given the financial pressure on the business.

Bought a handset through your site on 20 March (order reference 9X2-3347) and the device is misbehaving straight away. It's a Pixel 7a and since first charge the screen randomly flickers and the phone gets hot during light use; battery falls from 100% to 35% within three hours with no apps running. I'm tech literate and have tried safe mode, a factory reset and a fresh SIM — same problem. This is the first time I'm reporting it and I want either a replacement handset or an express repair appointment within five working days. Please advise whether you will post a pre‑paid return label and confirm turnaround time; I need a reliable device for work calls, so a loan phone would be useful while this is sorted.

Hello — I’m a student and I’ve got no broadband at my flat since 03 March, so I’m writing about it for the first time. I need to be online for a coursework submission next week and I can’t rely on my phone’s tethering for hours of research. I haven’t spoken to your support team yet; this is the initial notification. Please can you book an engineer visit that fits within the next 24 hours or provide a temporary 4G hotspot at no charge until the line is fixed. A quick confirmation by email or text would be appreciated so I can plan my study schedule.

I have been a customer of yours for over a decade (account opened 02/03/2012). On 08/03/2026 my handset — a basic 3G-only device used for medical monitoring — lost service entirely when your network shut down 3G in my area. Since that time I have had no voice or emergency-call capability. I informed your contact centre on 09/03/2026 (case ref 26-0389, adviser Karl H) and was given vague assurances that my SIM would be swapped; that has not occurred. I then phoned again on 11/03/2026 and spoke to Sonia in Technical Support who confirmed service withdrawal but could offer no immediate remedy and would not commit to a timescale. I have been forced to rely on neighbours and a battery-powered landline connection, which is not a long-term solution given my health needs. As a long-standing customer you owed me proper notice and a workable alternative. Under the Consumer Rights Act and Ofcom’s rules on service continuity and vulnerable customers, you are required to take reasonable steps to prevent exactly this outcome. You have failed to do so. I have exhausted ordinary complaint routes and now require escalation to a senior manager and a formal referral to the Communications Ombudsman if this is not resolved within 24 hours. I demand either immediate provision of an alternative handset/SIM that restores voice and emergency-call capability, or an immediate pro-rata refund of my contract charges plus a written explanation of why I was not given adequate notice and support. If you do not respond with an actionable solution today, I will commence a formal complaint to the Ombudsman and seek compensation through the small claims track for additional costs and distress.

Switched from another provider on 1 March. No updates. Contacted complaints line twice: 8 March (Liam) and 18 March (Priya). Both said ‘engineer will call’ and nothing. I have exhausted standard routes. Escalate to a manager and provide the ombudsman contact details if you cannot supply a progress update within five working days.

I switched over from my previous provider on 1 April and the package was meant to be active from that day. The router shows no sync and I was told by Tom in sales that an engineer named Mark would call to arrange an installation. Today is 6 April and nobody has called — the online tracker still says 'engineer scheduled' with no date. I work from home and cannot do my job without the line. This is the first time I'm reporting it but I need a proper update now: confirm whether an engineer will attend today or tomorrow and give me a time window. If you can't, escalate to someone who can. I chose your company for reliability; at the moment I'm left without the basics.

My phone stopped connecting to anything since 20/03/2026 when 3G was switched off in my area. I’m a student on a tight budget and rely on intermittent mobile data for banking apps and coursework when campus Wi‑Fi is down. No prior calls. Please advise how you will restore usable service or provide a reasonable alternative and tell me what the bill impact will be; I need this sorted within three working days.

I am trying to cancel my contract because my condition means I cannot manage ongoing calls from strangers. I phoned on 15/03 and spoke to Sarah who said she would send the cancellation form but nothing arrived (reference 56321). This is my second attempt. Cancel the service and confirm final bill date by 30 March. I need written confirmation to my email address. Do not add further charges.

I recently ported my number to your service on 25 January 2026 and on 15 February the apparent 3G network change degraded the service so severely that the mobile data I purchased is no longer fit for purpose. I raised this previously on 10 February (reference SRN652134) and received no substantive resolution. Under the Consumer Rights Act 2015 and your own stated terms, services supplied must be of satisfactory quality and as described. They are currently neither.

I have attempted troubleshooting with your technical support on 11 and 14 February yet connectivity remains intermittent when using legacy 3G-only devices required by some of my field staff. As a recent transferee from another supplier I reasonably expected continuity; instead we face operational disruption. I therefore request the following remedies within seven days: (1) immediate provision of a compatible network fallback or a suitable device replacement at no additional cost; (2) a pro rata rebate for the period of impaired service; and (3) a clear statement of steps you will take to prevent recurrence. If you fail to resolve the matter promptly I will seek contract termination without penalty and consider escalating this to Ofcom or the ADR scheme.

I run a small consultancy from a townhouse office in Leeds. On 18 March the broadband failed completely at 07:40 and has been intermittent at best since then; from 20 March the line has been dead with no data at all. I have called three times, spoken to Sam in Tier 1 on 18 March who opened fault 548921, then to Li on 20 March who said an engineer would attend that day and it was rescheduled twice, and finally to an advisor called Karen on 23 March who confirmed there was a network-wide issue and gave me ticket 548921-M. Each time I was promised an ETA; none were kept. My clients expect remote meetings and I’ve lost two paid projects and had three meetings cancelled. I have invoices due next week and I cannot generate the work without connectivity. I have exhausted the usual channels — I’ve sat through two hours on hold, used web chat, and waited for an engineer who never turned up — and I cannot endure another day of being told “we’re looking into it.” This is time-critical: my employees are standing idle and my cashflow is collapsing. Please escalate this now to a manager and involve the Ombudsman if you cannot supply an engineer within 24 hours. I need a reliable solution and compensation for the lost revenue today, not promises.

I moved over to your service on 28 January 2026 and, since then, have been charged several fees that were not mentioned when I signed up. I have raised this matter on at least four occasions (calls on 02 February with Martina, 11 February with Jamal, email on 18 February and a chat on 04 March), but every response either repeats policy wording or promises a review that never materialises. Specifically: a "setup fee" of £39.99 that I was never informed about at signup, and a "line activation" charge of £14.50 applied to my first bill. When I queried these charges I was told they were standard; when I asked where these were disclosed on your website or my order confirmation I received no clear answer. I am asking politely but firmly for an itemised explanation of each charge, a copy of the terms that purportedly authorise them, and a refund of any fees that were not properly disclosed at the point of sale. I can wait for your review to be completed, but I expect a proper investigation and a substantive reply this time.

I am writing because my March bill is wrong and this has gone on despite several contacts. Account ending 4821. On 22 March you charged me £74.99 for a 'Premium Data Pack' that I never bought; my usual monthly fee is £39.99. I have now called three times over the past three weeks (2 March with Hannah in Billing, 10 March with Owen in Tech Support and 18 March via web chat with a supervisor called Priya). Each time I was told the team would 'investigate' and I'd receive an itemised CSV — I have not received anything useful. I use your online portal and APIs daily; I can tell from the records that there was no change to my plan on 21–22 March. Please reverse the £35 overcharge, credit the account for any interest or bank charges incurred, and provide the detailed transaction log and authorisation that supposedly triggered this extra fee. If I do not see a correction and a clear timetable within seven days, I will escalate through the formal complaints route and my bank. I am firm about the deadline; this is causing unnecessary financial juggling for me at work.

My name is Margaret Hargreaves and I am writing because I cannot make sense of the bill I received on 3 March 2026. I am not comfortable with computers and rely entirely on paper statements. On that bill there is a charge of £37.50 described as “service admin” and another of £12.99 for “equipment protection” which I never agreed to. I took out a simple phone and broadband package in January 2025 and was told there would only be the monthly subscription and a line rental. There was no clear explanation of these extra amounts when the salesman set up the account over the phone. I phoned your automated line and a recorded message directed me to the online account, which I cannot use. I then rang the number given on my paperwork on 10 March 2026 and was on hold for half an hour before the call was dropped. This is my first time raising the matter formally because I did not know where else to turn. Please explain precisely what these charges are, supply a breakdown in plain English by post, and remove any fees that were not explicitly agreed. I am puzzled and upset to see my limited pension being reduced by charges I do not understand. I need someone to write to me at my home address with a clear explanation and a corrected bill within seven days, otherwise I will have no choice but to seek advice from my local Citizens Advice office.

This is a formal escalation. Ticket 554321 (raised 02/03/2026) concerns a complete broadband outage at my address preventing me from working from home. An engineer visit was promised for 05/03 and again for 12/03; neither appointment occurred. I have exhausted the normal support channels and have spoken to two senior advisers (Aisha Khan on 13/03 and Daniel Patel on 18/03). The service-level commitments in your published terms have been breached and I am accruing demonstrable loss of income as I am required to attend client meetings in person because my home connection is unreliable.

I request the following without delay: a named manager to take ownership, a guaranteed engineer appointment within 24 hours, interim provision of a temporary high-speed connection (dongle or similar) while repairs are completed, and a detailed remediation plan. If you cannot resolve this within 48 hours I will refer the matter to the Ombudsman and consider contractual remedies. Please respond with a manager contact and binding timetable.

I am the registered account holder for the family plan (mobile numbers 07745 992104 and 07745 992105). On 20 March 2026 I discovered that the billing contact details associated with my children’s SIMs had been altered without authority; a different email and a partial address were added. I have not authorised any change. This constitutes an unauthorised account modification and a potential data handling failure under the Data Protection Act. Your apparent inability to prevent or detect this is a serious compliance concern and places my children at risk of SIM fraud and loss of service for emergency calls. I require immediate reinstatement of the correct contact details, a full audit log of who made the change and when, and written confirmation of the corrective steps you will take to secure the account within 24 hours. If you fail to remedy this promptly I will notify the Information Commissioner’s Office and Ofcom and seek redress through the courts and the Ombudsman. Please treat this as an urgent protection-of-children matter.

I have been a customer since 2009 and I am formally raising a complaint regarding your automated support system's failure to provide a lawful remedy. On 18 March 2026 I initiated a query via your chat assistant (transcript ID CH-456789) about persistent packet loss on my broadband; the bot made three generic suggestions and terminated the session without offering a human agent or an escalation path. I attempted again via your web form on 22 March 2026 and received an automated acknowledgement only. As a long-standing subscriber I expect services and consumer protections set out in the Consumer Rights Act 2015 to be honoured. The absence of meaningful human intervention has left a partial outage affecting home working and, given the information your automated tool gathered, constitutes an inadequate remedy under your own contract terms.

I require the following within five working days: a named point of contact, a written technical assessment, and either a scheduled engineer visit or a clear refund/credit proposal proportionate to the degraded service since 12 March 2026. If no substantive response is received by 31 March 2026 I will refer the matter to Ofcom and consider seeking redress through the small claims process. Please escalate this to a senior complaints handler; I do not accept further automated replies.

I switched to you on 3 April and have been charged an early termination fee of £295 for a contract I ended before switching. I did not authorise any charge. I expect this reversed by close of business today and confirmation sent to my email. If not, I will block the payment through my bank and lodge a formal complaint with the regulator.

I am making a formal second request for immediate waiver of the early termination charge applied to my account (A/C 842179). I first raised this matter on 4 March 2026 with Mr Patel in your retention team and was given no substantive response — simply a promise someone would call back. I have not received that call. I am registered disabled with a mobility impairment and attend weekly hospital treatment. My clinician advised that my move to sheltered accommodation was essential and unavoidable; therefore terminating the contract early was a medical necessity. The invoice dated 9 March 2026 for an early termination fee of £275 is both unexpected and financially damaging. I provided medical evidence on 11 March to your vulnerabilities team (case reference VUL-1123) and was told it would be considered. To date there has been no decision communicated. This is a sensible request: remove the fee in light of documented health reasons or explain in writing, with legal basis, why you will not. I require a written resolution within seven days, otherwise I will have no option but to seek independent advice and escalate to the ombudsman. Please escalate this to a manager and confirm receipt of the medical documentation immediately.

Hello — I run Harper & Sons floristry on King Street and our broadband dropped out on the morning of 23/03. This is the first time I'm contacting you about it. Since then our card terminal and online ordering have been down; we lost paid orders during Mother's Day and the till can't process contactless, so we're losing trade and turning customers away. I estimate about £500 lost in one day and the uncertainty is killing the business. I can be reached between 08:00 and 18:00 today on the landline (which is dead) or mobile 07932 441 905 — please call that mobile within two hours of receiving this so we can arrange an emergency engineer or a temporary mobile backup. If a technician cannot attend the same day, I need a loaned router or a clear, immediate workaround so we can at least take payments. This is urgent; our livelihood depends on getting trade back on the tills today.

Dear Customer Services, I am writing on behalf of GreenLeaf Consultancy Ltd (account 78-44291) to dispute an incorrect charge on invoice 2026-03-15 for £342.50, issued 15 March. The item described as "equipment rental" should not have been billed: our contract (signed 01 February 2025) includes hardware at no extra cost for the duration of the fixed term. I raised this matter on 22 March with Sarah Martin (case reference 87421) and was told the billing team would investigate; I have received no update. This overcharge is affecting cash flow for my business; please credit £342.50 and issue a corrected invoice within 14 days of this letter. If further information is required, contact me by email; otherwise I expect the credit to appear on the April statement.

I am following up on a complaint raised on 21 March (ticket reference C-212089) about persistent outages on our business broadband and office VoIP line. My company, Oak & Iron Ltd, is a small distributor and these services are lifelines; on 19–22 March we missed three supplier deadlines, one court-mandated delivery window, and have received several contractual penalty notices. I spoke to your agent Rebecca Miles on 21/03 who promised investigation and a timeline but we received nothing substantive and the line dropped again on 24/03. This is my second attempt to get a proper remedy. The outage appears to be on your infrastructure and is not intermittent; there is a complete loss of service for several hours at a stretch. We are accruing verified financial losses and client claims. I require the following within 24 hours: a direct escalation to your business continuity team, a clear date and time for full restoration, interim measures (temporary dedicated connection or a mobile failover solution) so our customers can reach us, and written confirmation of your proposed commercial redress for losses incurred to date. If you cannot resolve this promptly I will file a complaint with the Telecommunications Ombudsman and seek compensation for tangible business losses plus any additional costs from sourcing alternative connectivity.

My small business has been without broadband and landline since 03 March 2026. I reported the outage at 09:20 on 03 March (case number 881233) and escalated to supervisor Priya Singh at 14:00 after an hour with no progress. Since then I have experienced total loss of service for 22 days. That outage has prevented us from taking orders, processing card payments and fulfilling contractual delivery commitments. To date I have suffered an estimated £6,400 in lost revenue and have been threatened with penalties from a client for missed SLA obligations. Repeated calls have produced only automated updates and a promise of engineer attendance that never occurred. Under the Consumer Rights Act and your own SLA, you are obliged to provide continuous service or reasonable compensation. I require immediate restoration of service, a detailed root‑cause report within 48 hours, and interim reimbursement for verified losses to date. If the service cannot be restored within 24 hours I will instruct my solicitor to seek injunctive relief and will notify Ofcom and the small business ombudsman of contractual breaches and regulatory non‑compliance.

Hi — putting this straightforwardly because I don't have time for long-winded customer service loops. My home broadband dropped completely on 24 March at 08:10 and I couldn't get online for work. I tried the online troubleshooting which suggested an engineer appointment; then the appointment was cancelled by text an hour before. I called, spoke with Callum on the support line at 10:05 who said an engineer would now be out 'within 48 hours' and set up a temporary mobile hotspot on my account. Forty-eight hours is not acceptable. I have two client meetings and an exam on Friday; if this isn't fixed by tomorrow morning I lose income and face professional consequences. This is my first time complaining but it's already urgent. Please escalate to the technical duty manager, send an engineer who can fix the line today, or provide a reliable replacement with static IP so I can work remotely. If you need proof of lost earnings I will provide it, but I need action first. Call me now and tell me exactly who is being sent and when.

I run a small catering business and rely on your line for card payments and online orders. On the invoice dated 14/02/2026 (Invoice 2026-041) there are three items marked as "administration fee" totalling £59.97 and an unexplained "connection service charge" of £120. I never consented to these fees when the contract was signed in January 2025, and they do not appear in the contract copy I hold (signed by your sales rep Daniel Clarke). I have contacted support repeatedly: emailed accounts on 18/02, called on 22/02 and spoke to Sophie in billing on 05/03 who promised to investigate and refund anything incorrectly applied. On 15/03 I escalated to Mark, who said the matter would be sent to the accounts manager. Nothing has been refunded, and the latest debit on 20/03 took my account into overdraft, causing my merchant terminal to be temporarily suspended — I lost three confirmed orders that day. I have been living with a chronic condition for several years and rely on my landline for telecare calls. Since your last repair promise the line has been unreliable. My student budget does not stretch to paying for a service that doesn't work. I ordered your new 5G mobile plan on 11 March 2026 specifically because you advertised guaranteed provisioning within 48 hours and guaranteed roaming/data fallback. I am a paying customer and I need a same‑day fix or a guaranteed fallback (temporary MiFi device) dispatched within 24 hours. If you cannot deliver, I expect a written escalation to a senior network engineer and a compensation offer. Time is critical — I cannot afford further delay.

I have been a customer since 2012 (account number 003-771-224) and am formally requesting cancellation of my broadband and line rental with effect from 30 April 2026. Phone cut out completely on 12 March and the complaint line has been useless. I have called three times since then. Reference numbers I was given are 781205, 781398 and 782001. I am contacting you regarding a faulty handset supplied as part of our family plan on 05 March 2026. The device is a Samsung Galaxy S21, IMEI 356789012345678. Account number ending 4821. I have been a customer for over a decade and raised this matter previously on 17 March 2026 with Raj Patel in technical support. I have been a customer of your network for over a decade and I am contacting you because the automated chat assistant has repeatedly failed to address what should be a straightforward enquiry. I moved into a shared house while studying and thought signing up with your student discount would be straightforward. It has not been. I've been with you for over a decade (signed up in 2012) and I do not expect to be chasing obvious billing errors. I switched from BrightNet to your service on 1 February and cancelled my previous provider on the same day. Switched from my previous provider on 4 February. Since then every time I call your support I'm put on hold for between 40 and 70 minutes. I subscribed to your 'Fibre 75' package on 1 March 2026. If no satisfactory response is received within 14 days I will escalate to Citizens Advice and the Telecommunications Ombudsman and consider further legal options to enforce my rights.

Wonderful timing to switch off 3G, clearly. My fallback handset only works on 3G and it is the device I use for my personal alarm and to contact my carers. If I am unable to reach emergency services because of your upgrade, that is a life-risk. Fix this immediately: provide a temporary 4G/VoLTE-capable device, or restore emergency connectivity to my number today, and send a technician to my address on 26 March. I have been a customer for just over twelve years and I am setting out the sequence of events because it illustrates why the current billing is both unexpected and unacceptable. I am asking for an immediate reversal of the incorrect charges, written confirmation that my account is closed, and an explanation of why the billing system continued to bill following confirmed cancellation. I am willing to discuss this by phone but prefer written confirmation of the outcome. If this is not resolved promptly I will have to consider further steps.

I am 72 and not very good with smartphones, but I can tell when the service is failing. For the past three months, calls drop and 4G disappears in my bungalow on Foxglove Close, BN3 8RT. I am contacting you regarding excessive waiting times when trying to reach support. As the account holder for our Family Plan (Account 8745612) I need this matter addressed promptly. Please confirm within five working days what steps you will take to reduce call-waiting times and how you will compensate for the time lost. I expect a clear timeline for improvement and a direct contact point so future calls are handled more promptly.

I am a paying customer who reads the small print. I signed up to your 'Flex Pro' tariff on 3 January 2026 because the promotional page promised unlimited calls and data and no additional fees beyond the monthly rate. My first bill, dated 1 February 2026, includes a mobile protection fee of 7.99 and a so-called 'service admin charge' of 4.50. Neither were disclosed at sign-up. I phoned billing on 10 February (agent name: Claire) who said the charges were legitimate and pointed me to the same PDF. I then used live chat on 17 February and spoke to Mark, who said he would escalate; nothing happened. I have kept screenshots, the signup page, and the exact timestamps of each call and chat. This is not an oversight. As someone who works in software I can say with certainty that fees that are hidden in mismatch documentation are effectively undisclosed. I demand an itemised explanation of each extra charge, a refund of anything not explicitly agreed at signup, correction of the online sign-up flow so future customers are not misled, and to speak with a manager or have this referred to the Ombudsman if it is not resolved within 14 days. I am not interested in being fobbed off with automated messages; provide evidence or remove the charges.

Hi — I'm Tom Li, and my mobile data has been intermittently dropping for the past two weeks. I raised a ticket through the app on 08/03/2026 (ticket #987654) after seeing download speeds fall to 0.2 Mbps while on 4G in central Manchester — after running multiple speedtests and swapping SIMs between two phones the problem looks network-side. Please provide a technical status update, the expected resolution timeline, and whether this will be prioritised. I would like a response within 48 hours with either a fix or a scheduled engineering action.

I am a young professional and I raise this as a principled complaint about your reliance on an AI assistant that fails to satisfy basic consumer protections. Under the Consumer Rights Act and your published terms, customers are entitled to accurate explanations of charges and an auditable trail of communications. I request a written account of the chatbot interaction, an explanation of how its answers are validated against contractual terms, and the name and direct contact of a complaints officer who can provide a binding clarification. I am not asking for immediate escalation, only that you remedy the informational deficiency and confirm within ten working days how I may obtain a formal correction to my account if required.

As a customer since 2012, I expect accurate information and an auditable record of interactions. I am disappointed that automated advice appears unreliable and wish to be reassured that this will not recur.

Yes, your system has honoured my calls about totally dropping service during my busiest trading hours. How considerate. My tills and phone rely on your line; this is causing daily revenue loss and I cannot continue to wait. I would appreciate you arranging an immediate site visit today, applying the agreed credit to my account, and providing a named contact who will take responsibility for ensuring the service remains stable. If a resolution is not provided by close of business I will be forced to pursue formal action via the regulator and seek compensation for lost earnings.

My business relies on your phone line. I called support on 9 March about the queues; after waiting 42 minutes I hung up. Between 2006 and now I have been a customer of yours; that history matters because I expected a different standard than the one I am receiving. On 10 March I raised a problem with repeated broadband dropouts that disconnect my home office VoIP and drop video calls; I spoke at length to Karen in Technical Support (case 84219). She promised an engineer would attend on 18 March and that a site visit would either identify a line fault or result in an upgrade to stabilise throughput. The engineer did not arrive. On 19 March I called again and was told by Ravi that an alternative appointment would be scheduled and that a senior ticket would be opened — there was no ticket number given and no follow-up email. I am now submitting this as my second formal request for a resolution because the promised fix has not materialised and the intermittent outages are costing me billable hours. For clarity, the situation is: dropouts occur roughly every 30–40 minutes, upload speeds collapse to under 0.1 Mbps during an outage, and a router swap on 12 March had no effect. I have run multiple speed tests (13 Mar: 36.2 Mbps down, 0.2 Mbps up; 17 Mar: 52.8 Mbps down, 0.1 Mbps up) and kept logs. I am asking for a proper engineering investigation, a guaranteed appointment window, and a clear timescale for a permanent remedy within seven days. If you cannot provide a lasting solution, I expect details of how you intend to compensate me for lost work and the steps you will take to prevent recurrence. I will not accept another round of reassurances that lead nowhere — I want an actionable plan and a date.

On 08 March 2026 I noticed an unknown charge of £156 on my account and my SIM stopped receiving texts. I had just moved to you on 01 March. I called your fraud team on 09 March and spoke with Liam, who opened a fraud ticket but said the charge was ‘pending review’ and to expect an update in seven days. Seven days passed with nothing. I then called again on 17 March; the agent I spoke to could see my complaint but could not confirm whether my number had been cloned or whether a third party had used it to take out services. I was advised to change passwords, which I had already done.

This feels like an attempted SIM swap or unauthorised account activity. I have had to put my personal and banking life on hold while this is investigated. I need a full forensic timeline of activity on my account from 1 March to present, an immediate reversal of the £156 if it proves unauthorised, and confirmation that my number is secure. I have already reported this to Action Fraud (reference AF-2026-112) and I am prepared to escalate to the ombudsman if your fraud team cannot provide a decisive response within five working days. Please assign a named investigator and give me a direct way to follow up — I cannot be left waiting again.

Phone sold as ‘fully working’ started cutting out on calls on 4 March and now the screen freezes and the mic fails completely, so I can’t make or receive anything. I’m a student with a tight budget; I can’t afford to buy another handset and I need to contact home and uni. The handset is still under warranty and you have my proof of purchase. Send a working replacement today, or confirm how you will cover the cost of a temporary loan phone so I can attend an assessment on Monday 30 March. This situation is urgent: missed deadlines and missed rent payments are now likely because I can’t access banking apps.

My business, Hartley & Co. Accountants (account 334455), depends on incoming client calls. Over the last two weeks I have experienced unacceptable call-waiting times when ringing your support and from clients calling our dedicated landline. On 22 March 2026 I timed three incoming calls: waits of 42, 51 and 48 minutes before reaching a human. As a result we lost six client appointments that day and estimated lost fees of approximately £600. I phoned your commercial support twice on 23 March and was placed on hold for 37 minutes both times. This is the first time I am raising the matter, but it cannot continue. My practice cannot operate if clients cannot reach us promptly. I require an explanation of why call handling has deteriorated, immediate steps to reduce queue times to acceptable levels, and compensation for demonstrable lost revenue. Please respond with a plan of corrective action and confirm what compensation you will offer within five working days.

I moved over from my previous provider because I wanted a straightforward unlimited data plan, and since 05/03/2026 I have been chasing the fact your system lists me on a different tariff. I have called three times — 07/03, 14/03 and this morning 22/03 — and spoke with Mark and then Leah, who both promised corrections would be made. The problem remains: my account shows a 200GB cap and a premium channel bundle I did not order, and I have received a bill for an extra £28.50 this month. I’m not looking for a lecture; I just want the plan I signed up for applied to my account, the incorrect charges refunded, and email confirmation that the cap and unwanted extras have been removed. Please action this and confirm by return so I can stop checking my account every day.

I purchased the family plan promoted online on 5 January 2026 and accepted the introductory offer that was clearly advertised as a 12-month deal at £22.99 per month. The customer service representative who processed the order, Claire, confirmed over the phone on 7 January 2026 that the contract would not auto-renew into any additional bundles and that I would receive a reminder email 30 days prior to the end of the promotional period. There was no mention of automatic conversion to a higher tariff.

On 15 March 2026 I noticed that my account was billed at £29.99 and, upon checking, the plan had converted to a different tariff labelled as ‘Standard Plus’ with no prior notification other than a single line in the T&Cs which I could not reasonably have spotted at the point of sale. This is the first time I am raising the issue. I would like a full explanation of why the account was transitioned without explicit consent, copies of any notifications sent prior to charging, and the exact clause in the contract that you rely on to justify auto-renewal at a higher price. I require the newly applied charge to be reversed and my account returned to the original promotional rate unless you can provide clear proof that I agreed to the change.

In addition, please clarify how future renewals will be handled. I am a parent with three dependent lines on this account; unexpected changes to standing charges are disruptive. I ask for evidence of the notification trail (dates, times, recipient email addresses or SMS content), and a named contact who will confirm when the refund has been actioned. I will wait for your comprehensive response, but I expect this rectified without delay.

Hi — this is my first time raising this but I need some help. I am registered disabled and have recently found my circumstances mean I can no longer reliably use the phone package I signed up to. I entered a 24-month contract on 1 January 2026, but due to a change in my health I have been advised by my GP to limit travel and reduce appointments outside the home. That makes the contract unsuitable and I need to cancel.

I can provide an NHS letter or GP note if required. Before I go through the formal cancellation route I would like to know whether you will waive the early termination charge of £245 in these circumstances and, if not, what documentation you require. I’m hoping for a practical solution that recognises my situation. Please advise the next steps and how to submit medical evidence. This is the first time I have raised this matter with you, so if there is an internal concessions team please put me in touch with them.

I am writing as the named account holder for a family plan (account number ending 1842). Over the past three weeks I have repeatedly used your automated assistant, 'HelpBot', hoping to resolve a billing query about a £9.99 charge posted on 15 March 2026. On 16 March, 20 March and 24 March the chatbot provided inconsistent answers, incorrectly stating that the charge was for a separate line and then claiming it was a pro rata adjustment. On 24 March the bot refused to escalate to a human adviser, citing policy, and would not supply a transcript of the interaction when requested. I am a parent managing multiple lines; this level of service is unacceptable. Under the Consumer Contracts Regulations and the relevant provisions of the Consumer Rights Act 2015 I am entitled to clear, accurate information about charges and a proper means of redress. I therefore request: (1) a full itemised breakdown of the £9.99 charge dated 15 March 2026; (2) the complete chat transcripts for the sessions on 16/03, 20/03 and 24/03; and (3) immediate escalation to a named human adviser with authority to correct billing errors. If you do not provide these within 14 days I will consider formal escalation to Ofcom and the Telecommunications Ombudsman. I can wait for a formal investigation, but I require assurance you will not continue to rely on an automated system that denies customers access to legitimate remedies.

I am a full-time student on a tight budget and I am writing after exhausting your standard channels without resolution. Over the last three months my bills have included successive undisclosed charges described as 'equipment handling' (£8.99 per month) and a retrospective 'paper invoice fee' (£6.00). Each time I have been told the fees are 'standard' but I never consented to or was informed of them at contract formation. Under the Consumer Contracts (Information, Cancellation and Additional Charges) Regulations 2013 and the Consumer Rights Act 2015 you are obliged to provide clear pre-contract information about additional sums payable. I formally request repayment of £44.96 (three months of the two fees), a written undertaking that no further undisclosed fees will be applied, and an explanation of how these charges were added to my account. If I do not receive satisfactory resolution within seven working days, I will escalate to the Communications Ombudsman and refer the matter to Ofcom for breach of complaint-handling obligations.

I have been with this company for over a decade and have always tried to be reasonable, which is why I am contacting you now. I bought an iPhone 13 from your online store on 1 February (order 112233) and the handset will not charge from the supplied cable or wirelessly. Please advise the practical next step: repair, replacement or collection. I would like this arranged within seven working days as I rely on my phone for both work and family contact. If you need any additional details, the serial number ends 78K9.

I am a vulnerable customer with reduced mobility and I am submitting this as a follow-up to my previous complaint raised on 02/03/2026 with reference CR-20260302-41. Under the Consumer Rights Act and the terms of your own fair treatment policy, I require an immediate reimbursement of the two charges totalling £129.98 and written confirmation that no further sums will be taken. I also request written assurance that my account closure has been logged and that my personal data will not be used for marketing following cancellation. I raised this matter once before and received a holding response that did not resolve the refund. I am constrained in mobility and rely on clear written correspondence; please send a full statement of account to my home address and an email confirmation. If you do not provide a full refund and written confirmation within 14 days I will pursue alternative dispute resolution and will seek advice on my statutory rights.

We run a small catering business and you are our primary communications provider. On 09/03/2026 the line dropped during an order window and since then call reliability has been poor. As a result we missed two supply orders and lost roughly £1,200 in revenue this month — I have invoices and timestamps to prove it. Your complaints process has been ineffective and dismissive; no detailed investigation has been provided and no manager has contacted us despite repeated requests. If you cannot deliver that within five working days please prepare for a formal complaint to the ombudsman and for me to pursue recovery of actual losses.

On 12 March 2026 I discovered that my mobile number had been effectively transferred and several unauthorised charges appeared on my account totalling 320.00. I am a student living on a constrained budget; the loss is material and I need this corrected and refunded immediately.

I raised the problem with your fraud team on 13 March and received complaint reference 54321. I require the following without delay: a freeze on any further changes to my account, immediate reversal of the 320.00 in unauthorised charges, delivery of the full call and access logs related to my number for 11–13 March, and confirmation that you will liaise with Action Fraud and my bank. If you cannot provide confirmation and a refund within 24 hours I will report the matter to Action Fraud and escalate to Ofcom and my university’s legal advice service. Please treat this as high priority and contact me by phone on the number registered to the account.

I’ve been with you since 2008 and I am beyond tired of this. I’ve raised this multiple times; I expect one clear answer and a matching offer, not another vague promise to ‘look into it’.

I am a young professional and I do not have time for unclear billing. I expect an itemised explanation, immediate reversal of any unjustified sums and a written apology. Please respond with a clear breakdown of each charge and a refund timetable.

I am Mrs Joan Hargreaves and I need an immediate correction to my account. I cancelled my Pay Monthly SIM on 9 March 2026 by telephone because I no longer wanted the line. I am writing to request immediate confirmation that my order has been cancelled. This is my first time raising the matter with you; please treat this as a formal request to confirm cancellation in writing, and to supply a final statement showing zero balance. I expect written confirmation by 1 April 2026 and a clear explanation of why your system still shows activity when the cancellation was submitted on 07 March. If you require further information from me to complete this, tell me precisely what is needed and do not redirect me to general help pages.

Switched from my previous provider on 10 March. Line has been dead since 12 March. Raised once on 13 March; ticket 554210 but no repair arranged. This is urgent — I need service restored immediately or to be moved back to my old provider. Please contact me with an engineer slot today.

When I switched to your service on 1 January I signed an 18-month contract under the clear terms of £35.00 per month, account number 54-3102. I require a written justification for the mid-contract change, a reversal of the March overcharge of £7.50 pending that justification, and an undertaking that either the contract terms will be honoured for the duration or that I may exit the agreement without penalty. Please escalate this to a senior account manager and provide full account transaction details for my review within five working days.

Account number 88765421. I switched from my previous provider on 15 February 2026. My first bill (dated 01 March 2026) shows a charge of £48.99 when my plan was advertised as £28.99. That is a £20 discrepancy. No discounts, no usage beyond allowance. This is my first time raising the issue. Please correct the bill, apply the proper rate, and confirm the corrected amount by email with a revised invoice.

I want to tell you why this feels unfair. For twenty years I paid for the same broadband package and, because I live with chronic rheumatoid arthritis, predictable bills matter to me; I budget carefully. Last month I discovered that a new customer promotion offered the same speed for £10 less than I’m being charged. I raised this with an agent called Tom on 09 March and he promised someone would review my account; nothing happened. I’m not throwing accusations around — I’m explaining the situation: I stayed loyal through periods when my mobility made moving provider difficult, because I trusted you would value long-standing customers. Instead I feel penalised for staying put. I don’t want to sound confrontational, but I do want parity or an explanation of why returning customers are given better rates than those who stayed. Please review my account (account name: Elaine Turner, account number 77-1984) and tell me what you can offer that recognises my circumstances. If there is a reason I’m being charged more, explain it clearly and plainly — I had to call a taxi to get to a shop once because of flare-ups, I shouldn’t now be paying a premium for the convenience I relied on for years. I raised this once before and was told someone would call back; I’m still waiting.

This is my second attempt to obtain a proper answer about the chatbot failing to submit a complaint and the subsequent lack of follow-up. I raised the matter on 14 March (support ticket 20260314-55) and received only an automated response. Under the Consumer Rights Act 2015 and relevant Ofcom guidance, I expect businesses to provide clear records of complaints and to respond within a reasonable timeframe. On 16 March I tried the automated assistant again; it acknowledged the issue but then dropped the session and produced no reference number. I am a student with limited funds and cannot accept unresolved billing or service queries lingering without documentation. I request that you log this message as a formal complaint, provide a substantive response within 10 working days, and issue a complaint reference. If you require additional information I will supply call and chat timestamps, but please do not return another generic bot message. I am aware of my rights and will seek further recourse if necessary, though I am hopeful you will address this promptly and properly.

To the Complaints Team,

I switched from my previous provider on 1 February and have been unable to resolve a simple billing query because your automated system is ineffective. Between 10 February and 14 March I used the chatbot on your website on four separate occasions; each time it returned contradictory information about prorated charges and then closed the session without producing a transcript. I then attempted to use the live chat on 18 March and was transferred twice before being told the bot had already explained everything. The transcript reference provided by the agent (ref: CB-0218-779) is incorrect.

I have now exhausted the normal channels. I request that a senior advisor or manager with authority over billing reviews my account (Account name: Eleanor Cruz, account number 77-334-221) and issues a clear, written explanation of the charges for the period 1–28 February, including any prorations. If you cannot provide an accurate resolution within 10 working days I will escalate this to the Ombudsman for Communications. I am disappointed at how an apparently straightforward matter has been handled and expect better clarity from a company I chose to move to.

Hi, I’m Mark Singh and I look after our family plan. I signed up to a two-year contract on 3 February 2026 because I was told switching would be straightforward and that devices would work across your network. It turns out that the handset my partner needs for work is incompatible and, when I asked to cancel within a week, your automated system hit me with an early termination charge of £285 per line — that’s crippling for us right now. I’ve got kids in nursery and bills to cover; this charge is not only unfair, it’s actively causing financial distress. Please escalate to someone who can actually fix billing errors today and call me back on the number on file.

I run a horticultural supplies business, D. Meadows Limited (registered), and our mobile terminals on several sites rely on your 3G connections for card payments. Since your announced 3G switch-off we have been unable to process payments at two sites, which directly stops us trading. I have contacted your support repeatedly—calls on 04/03 (agent Mark Reynolds, ticket 77421), 11/03 (engineer promise, Sarah), and 18/03 (senior advisor, Lewis) — each time told a solution was forthcoming within 48–72 hours, and each time nothing changed. This is my fourth contact within three weeks. We are losing revenue each trading day and customers are leaving because we cannot accept cards; last weekend alone we lost an estimated £3,200 in sales. I am asking, politely but firmly, for an engineer on site within 24 hours and either immediate provision of a compatible 4G router or temporary replacement devices so we can take payments. If you cannot supply that, arrange immediate porting to an alternative provider at your cost. I do not want to repeat these calls every few days. Please escalate this now to your field operations manager and give me a time and a contact who will see it through.

Look, I run a little mobile bike-repair service and my payment terminals and parts scanners are still on 3G SIMs. On 10 March my machines lost connectivity because your 3G network was shut down earlier than I'd been told. I’ve rung support four times since—last week with Jake, the day after with Priya—and each time I’ve been told different things about migration options and that a technician would call. Nobody’s called back. I can still take cash but card sales are a big chunk of my takings; this is making a mess of the next few days’ income. I just need a clear plan: either provide a 4G replacement SIM that works with my terminals by 01 April or credit my account for lost card fees until you do. I’m frustrated at being passed around; please sort this quickly.

I run a small photography business and poor indoor signal in my studio has become a persistent problem. I first reported dropped calls and patchy data inside 28 High Street on 10 January; the agent, Tom, logged a complaint and said an engineer would check local coverage. Nothing happened. I chased on 24 January and then again on 7 February; each time I was given a different explanation — congestion, a temporary mast fault, then a suggestion I switch to 4G only — and told someone would escalate to network engineering. There have been at least four contacts in total. On 18 February I had a call with Jas from technical support who booked a site survey for 3 March; nobody turned up and the survey was marked completed in the notes. The consequence is that I cannot reliably take booking calls from clients when I am in the studio; I have missed two tentative bookings because calls dropped, which is irksome and worrying. This is not catastrophic — payments and accounting are unaffected — but it is damaging to my reputation and causes unnecessary stress. I have tried everything short of changing premises. I require a proper local radio survey, a clear explanation of why appointments are being marked as completed when no engineer attends, and a timeline for any remedial action (signal booster or mast work). I will wait for a substantive reply, but I expect you to stop treating this as minor noise and give me one accountable contact to resolve it.

I have been a customer of your company for eleven years and have always paid on time. I am writing about an automatic renewal that took place without my explicit consent on 1 March 2026. My plan, Unlimited Plus (monthly price normally £29.99), was renewed and a charge of £34.99 appeared on my debit card on that date for the 12-month renewal. I did not receive a separate notification advising that the contract would roll over, nor did I authorise a renewal by phone or online. I raised this once previously via email (5 March) and was told someone would investigate; that was two weeks ago and nothing further has been communicated. As a long-standing customer I find the lack of clarity and the failure to process a refund acceptable. I would like a full breakdown of why the renewal occurred, the specific clause in your terms you believe permits charging without a notice, and immediate refund of the £34.99 pending full review. If there was an administrative error on your end, please confirm corrective action and offer an appropriate retention gesture; if you believe the charge is legitimate, provide the evidence that I consented in writing.

Please acknowledge receipt of this message and give a clear timeline for resolution. I am prepared to wait a reasonable number of days for you to investigate, but I expect a proper, written response rather than another automated message.

Sincerely,
Margaret Collins

I’m writing because my March bill is wrong and I’ve already been through support once. On 18 March you billed me £112.99 for mobile service when my plan is the 30GB Young Pro at £32.99 — the invoice shows a mysterious ‘Premium Data Pack’. I spoke to Sam on 20 March (reference 987612) who assured me the extra charge would be removed; nothing has changed and you’ve now taken the money from my account. I don’t have time for back-and-forths. Correct the bill, refund £80 to the card used on 18 March and send an itemised statement within five working days. If this isn’t sorted quickly I’ll port my number and leave a full review. I expect confirmation of the refund by email today.

Phone lines: disgraceful. Spent 47 minutes on 22/03/2026 listening to hold music. No advisor. No call-back. I have tried chat twice and the app offered no help. I have exhausted your usual channels. Escalate this to a manager now or I will be taking the matter to the Ombudsman for persistent access failures.

I switched to your service less than a month ago and had expected at least basic accessibility to your support. Instead, what I have experienced is an absurdly long and unpredictable telephone queue that is already costing me time and money. On 24 March 2026 I rang at 09:17 to query an installation slot and was told the wait would be around 20 minutes; I was held for 42 minutes before the line cut out. At 16:03 on 25 March I called again and the IVR loop replayed the same options for ten minutes before a message announced the expected wait was 45–60 minutes; I waited 51 minutes and abandoned the call. I then used web chat on 25 March at 18:12 and stayed connected for 35 minutes answering automated prompts, only to be disconnected without explanation. The pattern is the same: long waits, repeated disconnections, no human contact when promised. This matters because the missed appointments have left my household without broadband during work hours and disrupted a contractor's installation visit. I accept that occasional delays happen, but the total lack of reliable access to support is unacceptable for a paying customer. Please investigate what is causing these queue failures, provide an explanation of the steps you will take to reduce wait times, and arrange a guaranteed callback within three working days at a time I specify. I also expect a goodwill gesture for lost working time and the cost of rearranged appointments. If you cannot arrange a callback, explain why and give me the name and contact details of someone senior who will take responsibility for resolving this.

I rely on this line for emergency calls and cannot be left without it — please arrange an engineer visit today and tell me how to escalate if you cannot fix it immediately.

The installation appointment was booked for 17/02/2026 and the porting of my number was due to complete on that day. Since then there has been no substantive communication: the last update on the portal was 17/02/2026 and I have had two calls to your helpdesk (Kate on 23/02, case 778811-23K) and a complaints email reply on 03/03/2026 that offered no timeline. I have now exhausted the normal channels and require escalation to a manager or the Ombudsman if you cannot supply a firm plan immediately. Under the standards expected of new customer migrations, you are obliged to keep me informed of progress and any delays; your silence has left me unable to confirm the switch with my contacts, and I have lost an evening of work waiting for the engineer who did not arrive. Please provide within 48 hours: (1) a confirmed date and two-hour slot for installation and number porting, (2) a precise explanation for the delay since 17/02/2026 and why I received no interim updates, and (3) details of compensation you will offer for the time and inconvenience caused. If you fail to respond with a satisfactory plan, I will take this further via alternative dispute resolution channels.

I run a small catering business and purchased ten company handsets and a business line from you on 12 January 2026 (account 44-19-221). Since 05 March 2026 multiple handsets show repeated SIM errors and no network registration. I have called support on 06 March and 11 March, spoken to Mark and then to Lydia, and completed every diagnostic your scripted agents suggested. The faults have rendered three of my delivery drivers unable to take orders or update customers. I have documented loss of earnings totalling £1,240. I have now exhausted the normal complaint routes and the issue remains. Under the Consumer Rights Act 2015 the goods supplied must be fit for purpose and of satisfactory quality. These handsets are clearly not. I am therefore formally requesting immediate remedial action: either full replacement of faulty devices within five working days or a pro rata refund for devices that fail to network, plus compensation for demonstrable loss. If you do not escalate this to a senior manager and provide a binding timetable by close of business on 29 March 2026 I will refer the matter to the Ombudsman and consider legal remedies to recover my losses. This is not a negotiation; it is a statement of my rights and the impact on my livelihood.
